status_detail: Green hydrogen production for TotalEnergies' Grandpuits biorefinery (Seine-et-Marne, France). Part of the zero-crude conversion of the Grandpuits refinery into a biofuel and bioplastics platform. Electrolyzer capacity not separately disclosed but integrated with the larger biofuel conversion project.

data_management.narrative_summary: TotalEnergies is converting its Grandpuits refinery in Seine-et-Marne, France, into a zero-crude biofuel and bioplastics platform. As part of this conversion, green hydrogen production via electrolysis will replace fossil-derived hydrogen for hydrotreating vegetable oils and producing sustainable aviation fuel (SAF). The electrolyzer is integrated with the broader EUR 500 million conversion project. Grandpuits represents a model for refinery conversion to biofuels combined with on-site green hydrogen production, leveraging TotalEnergies' broader hydrogen procurement strategy including Normand'Hy (GA-PR-001) and ELYgator (GA-PR-017).

sources.title: TotalEnergies Grandpuits zero-crude conversion project — green hydrogen integration
sources.author: TotalEnergies SE
